frameworks.name: implicit vs explicit emotion processing
frameworks.one_line: dissociates automatic reflexive emotion processing from deliberate cognitive evaluation

frameworks.name: functional connectivity analysis
frameworks.one_line: MPFC-centred network coupling indexes group differences beyond regional activation

frameworks.name: empathizing-systemizing account of ASD
frameworks.one_line: individual empathizing ability linked to brain response in autism

core_question: Do adults with ASD show altered brain activation and connectivity during implicit versus explicit emotion processing?
key_assumptions: implicit and explicit emotional processing recruit dissociable neural substrates
ASD difficulties are pronounced for subtle automatic emotional contexts
age and IQ matching isolates ASD-specific neural differences
manipulations: implicit versus explicit emotion processing task demands
group comparison: adults with ASD versus typically developing
key_findings: reduced MPFC and STG activation in ASD during implicit emotion processing
weaker MPFC functional connectivity with amygdala, temporal, parietal, and fusiform regions in ASD
no group difference in accuracy or reaction time
positive empathizing-STG activity relationship in ASD only
interpretations: implicit but not explicit emotion processing is altered at multiple neural levels in ASD
STG engagement may scale with empathizing capacity specifically in autism
